meta.title: The Morrow Doctrine
meta.subtitle: The Self-Authored Myth-Maker and the Republic That Armed Him
meta.coreStatement: America makes exceptional individuals possible, uses them, and destroys them when they become expensive.
meta.narrativeArgument: The gap between American ideals and American practice is wide enough to be genuinely generative — and genuinely fatal.

principles.name: The Conditional License
principles.definition: All individual greatness operates on institutional permission. The permission is always conditional, always revocable, and always revoked when the individual's value is exceeded by their cost.

principles.name: The Self-Portrait as Prison
principles.definition: The creator's project inevitably develops independence from its creator, because any sufficiently complex creation acquires its own logic. The creator experiences this independence as betrayal.

principles.name: The Closing Frontier
principles.definition: Exceptional individuals require exceptional environments. When those environments close, the exceptional individual becomes an anachronism reclassified as a threat.

principles.name: Mirror Blindness
principles.definition: The protagonist can diagnose decline and self-deception in every entity except themselves. Their clearest insight into others is powered by their blindest spot about themselves.

principles.name: Complicity & Liquidation
principles.definition: The state uses exceptional individuals for work it cannot acknowledge. When the work becomes expensive, it sacrifices the agent rather than admit its own role.

entities.name: Cassian Morrow
entities.psychology: Experiences limits as insults. Ambition as self-expression, not collective vision. Mirror blindness prevents self-diagnosis.

entities.name: Idris Caine
entities.psychology: Maintains the system because the alternative is worse. Cognitive equal, incompatible orientation.

entities.name: Solomon Eckhart
entities.psychology: Equal talent, capacity for satisfaction. Can stop. Enough.

entities.name: Asa Lorn
entities.psychology: Loyal to the person, not the project. Follows until following means becoming unrecognizable.

entities.name: Aurelius Dane
entities.psychology: Loves the creation as community, not self-expression.

entities.name: Lucan Verge
entities.psychology: Comparable skill, zero self-mythology. Honest mercenary.

entities.name: Thessaly
entities.psychology: Built as total self-expression; develops actual life creator cannot control.

entities.name: USOC
entities.psychology: Creates authority, uses capability, builds file, destroys.

relationships.dynamic: Genuine friendship, divergent capacity for satisfaction

acts.title: The Knight
acts.question: Who is Morrow at peak, and what is the hunger he cannot name?

acts.title: The Builder
acts.question: What happens when the Myth-Maker finds his canvas?

acts.title: The Appetite
acts.question: What happens when the hunger exceeds every container?

acts.title: The Fracture
acts.question: What happens when everything built develops its own will?

acts.title: The Execution
acts.question: What does the Myth-Maker do when the myth reaches its limit?

expressions.content: I can't be smaller than what I've seen.
The best of us, turned to purposes that were only ever his own.
expressions.note: Affirms greatness, identifies redirection, names selfishness.

expressions.content: I know. That's why I'm leaving.
I Am What You Celebrate — Confirmation → Pattern → Incomprehension → Haunting Return
Mirror motif — reflective surfaces track ego coherence.
expressions.note: Visual tracking of protagonist's disintegrating self-knowledge.

expressions.content: You call it piracy when I do it for myself. What do you call it when you do it through me?
